steps.title: Find the gesture (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch a long, shallow M — one low arc on the left, a slight dip in the middle, another low arc on the right. This single gesture line is the whole bird. Keep it loose.

steps.title: Mark the wing tips and body (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, place a small dot at each end of your M for the wing tips, and a tiny oval where the dip meets the middle for the body. These dots tell you where your ink line will start, bend, and end.

steps.title: Switch to ink and lay down the front edge
steps.description: Switch to ink. Starting from the left wing tip, draw one confident M-shaped stroke across the top of the wings, through the body, and out to the right wing tip. One pass — don't redraw.

steps.title: Ink the back edge of the wings
steps.description: Still in ink, draw a second curve underneath the first, mostly parallel but a touch flatter, closing the wing shapes. The body in the middle should bulge slightly between the two curves.

steps.title: Suggest the head (ink)
steps.description: Add a tiny bump on top of the body in the middle for the head, and a short angled mark for a beak pointing forward. Two small strokes is enough — at this distance the bird is mostly silhouette.
